recommendations: redundant_read_same_unit escalating trend (1→2→3 units across three consecutive cycles) warrants prioritization over other RECURRING:STABLE patterns; bc2715fa-33 three-read instance on code-block.tsx with no offset variation is the highest single-unit count recorded — a circuit-breaker heuristic (if about to Read the same path a second time within a unit without a different offset or limit, pause and use previously-read content) could interrupt this pattern before it reaches 4+ reads per unit

optimistic_write_without_read escalated from 1 to 2 units this cycle; both instances follow identical Read-confirms-existence-then-Write topology; a pre-Write check (test -f path before Write on an expected-new file) would eliminate the pattern at near-zero cost; since the write tool itself errors rather than silently corrupting, the blast radius is low but the thrash adds tool-slot waste

shell_tool_preference_violations and hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path have shown zero internalization across 40+ consecutive cycles — do not re-recommend structural fixes for these; the hook gap for absolute-path /usr/bin/grep is the load-bearing root cause and requires a hook-level fix, not behavioral change

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise self-documentation is executing correctly (bc2715fa-18 ritual is well-formed) but the violation reflex remains uncorrected; the self-documentation approach alone is insufficient across 5 measured cycles; a pre-completion gate (checklist item: 'have I started dev server and loaded a page exercising this code path?') embedded in task completion flow may be more effective than post-hoc mistake-pattern appends
